summary: Comprehensive testing of Inventory Balance CSV Export feature (Iteration 62). All 16 backend tests passed for GET /api/inventory-ledger/export endpoint. Frontend verified: Export CSV button visible with data-testid='inv-export-csv-btn', opens new tab with export URL. Balance table renders with history icons. All regression endpoints working.

issues: Console hydration warnings: '<tr> cannot be a child of <span>' - React hydration issue in table rendering (non-critical, cosmetic)

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/inventory-ledger/export?customer_id=X returns 200
Backend: Content-Type is text/csv; charset=utf-8
Backend: Content-Disposition has attachment; filename='inventory_Hormel_Foods.csv'
Backend: CSV headers are correct (10 columns: item, item_description, warehouse, ownership_type, on_hand, incoming, committed, available, unit_of_measure, status)
Backend: CSV contains 9+ data rows for Hormel Foods workspace
Backend: Status values are only OK/LOW/SHORT (validated all rows)
Backend: SHORT status correctly set for items with is_short=True (SPAM-LITE, TEST-ITEM-WARN, TEST-WARNONLY-732C83)
Backend: Item filter works - item=SPAM-12OZ returns 2 rows (GPI-MAIN and GPI-WEST)
Backend: Empty result (non-existent item) returns CSV with headers only, 0 data rows
Backend: Exported values match GET /customers/{id}/balances API (9 items)
Backend: Missing customer_id returns 422 validation error
REGRESSION: POST /api/inventory-ledger/movements (manual entry) still works
REGRESSION: GET /api/inventory-ledger/history returns movements (14 total)
REGRESSION: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order responds correctly
REGRESSION: POST /api/incoming-supply/from-shortage responds correctly
REGRESSION: GET /customers/{id}/balances still works
Frontend: Login with admin/admin successful
Frontend: Navigate to Inventory Ledger page loads correctly
Frontend: Customer selector shows Hormel Foods workspace
Frontend: Click Hormel Foods loads workspace view
Frontend: Export CSV button (data-testid='inv-export-csv-btn') visible in Balances tab
Frontend: Button text is 'Export CSV' with Download icon
Frontend: Click Export CSV opens new browser tab
Frontend: Balance table renders with STATUS column badges (SHORT=red, LOW=yellow, OK=green)
Frontend: History icons (ClipboardList) present on each row (data-testid='inv-history-btn-X')
critical_code_review_comments: Export endpoint uses derive_balances() - same pipeline as UI, ensures consistency
StreamingResponse with text/csv media type and Content-Disposition attachment
Filename sanitized: spaces → underscores, limited to 40 chars
Empty result correctly returns CSV with headers only (no error)
Frontend button uses window.open() to open export URL in new tab with customer_id and optional item filter
seed_data_used: Used existing Hormel Foods workspace (ID: 366bfcce-4d68-4daf-b9f4-cb05a35de8c8) with 10+ balance rows including SHORT/LOW/OK status items. Backend tests created TEST_EXPORT_REGRESS_* items for regression verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CSV Export feature fully tested. Key endpoint: GET /api/inventory-ledger/export?customer_id=X[&item=Y][&warehouse=Z]. Returns StreamingResponse with text/csv, Content-Disposition attachment. Headers: item, item_description, warehouse, ownership_type, on_hand, incoming, committed, available, unit_of_measure, status. Status derived from is_short/is_low flags. Frontend button data-testid='inv-export-csv-btn' opens window.open() to export URL. Balance table shows status badges and history icons per row.

query_params.item: optional - filter by exact item name

export_button.action: window.open() to export URL with customer_id and optional item filter

balance_table.history_icons: ClipboardList icon button on each row (data-testid='inv-history-btn-{index}')
